title: xanthophyllum erythrostachyum
intro: Xanthophyllum erythrostachyum, a species of flowering plant in the family Polygalaceae, is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia and is commonly known as the "red spike" or "red spiky fruit". It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Xanthophyllum erythrostachyum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are often used to treat wounds, skin infections, and other health conditions.
